headlines.text: US-Iran ceasefire frays further as CENTCOM shoots down more drones over Strait of Hormuz
Knicks lead NBA Finals 2-0 over Spurs; Game 3 Monday at MSG with Trump in attendance
Nasdaq suffers worst day since April 2025, drops 4.2% on chip rout and hot jobs report
Pope Leo XIV celebrates Corpus Christi Mass in Madrid on first papal visit to Spain in 15 years
Senate passes Trump immigration enforcement funding bill after weeks of bitter delays
At least 12 wounded in shooting near Toledo, Ohio street festival
US to redirect Iranian frozen assets to help Gulf allies rebuild from war damage
Peru holds presidential election: tight race between Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sanchez
Bernie Sanders introduces American AI Sovereign Wealth Fund Act targeting OpenAI and Big Tech
US men's soccer team beats Germany in World Cup tune-up ahead of FIFA 2026 kickoff June 11

breaking.text: CENTCOM: US forces shoot down Iranian drones threatening Strait of Hormuz; Iran calls it a ceasefire violation

stories.hed: Drones, Missiles, and a Ceasefire That Exists Mostly on Paper
stories.dek: The US shot down more Iranian drones threatening the Strait of Hormuz, Iran fired seven ballistic missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain, and both sides are accusing the other of violating a ceasefire they both claim to support. Meanwhile Trump says a deal could come 'this weekend' -- a sentiment Iran's foreign minister found hilarious.

stories.hed: Pope Leo XIV Draws Massive Crowds in Madrid for First Papal Visit to Spain in 15 Years
stories.dek: Pope Leo XIV presided over a Corpus Christi Mass at Plaza de Cibeles with tens of thousands cheering. It is the first papal visit to Spain in 15 years, and the second day of a seven-day apostolic tour of the country.

stories.hed: Senate Passes Trump Immigration Enforcement Funding Bill After Rocky Weeks
stories.dek: The Senate passed legislation funding Trump's immigration enforcement agencies after fierce backlash over an unrelated $1.776 billion settlement fund nearly derailed it. The bill now heads to Trump's desk, guaranteeing uninterrupted money for mass deportation operations.

stories.hed: Defense Intelligence Agency Raises Counterintelligence Threat Level Over Israeli Espionage Concerns
stories.dek: The DIA quietly raised its counterintelligence threat level in recent weeks amid growing concerns that Israeli espionage against the US had become more aggressive than usual, sources tell NBC News. Awkward timing given the two countries are literally fighting a war together.

stories.hed: US Blocked Visas for 15 Iranian World Cup Staff -- Iran's Soccer Team Might Show Up Literally Unmanaged
stories.dek: The US refused visas to 15 members of Iran's World Cup delegation, meaning the Iranian national team could arrive at the FIFA 2026 tournament -- being hosted on US soil -- without its full support staff. Iran is taking it to FIFA, which should make for a very normal and not at all complicated tournament.

markets.context: Markets tanked Friday after a blowout May jobs report (172k vs 80k expected) sent Treasury yields surging, crushed chip stocks, and reminded investors that the Fed may actually have to hike rates.

gas.note: Gas is $4.17/gal nationally -- which is roughly what a movie ticket cost in 2005, except now it just takes you to work and back and you feel nothing.

popCulture.hed: Knicks vs. Spurs NBA Finals Has NYC at Peak Hysteria -- MSG Game 3 Tickets Hitting $9,000
popCulture.dek: The New York Knicks lead the 2026 NBA Finals 2-0 over the San Antonio Spurs in a rematch of the 1999 Finals, and the city has completely lost its mind. Worst seats at Monday's Game 3 at Madison Square Garden were going for nearly $9,000 on the secondary market -- which is either insane or completely reasonable depending on whether you've waited 53 years for a title.

genZ.definition: Saxboy Billy's viral track 'The Puerto Rico Song' is the inescapable summer earworm dominating TikTok FYPs in June 2026, powering everything from beach POVs to World Cup hype clips. It blew up in the lead-up to FIFA 2026 and nobody can explain exactly why but everyone has heard it at least 40 times this week.
genZ.usage: POV: you're at the gas pump paying $4.17 a gallon while 'The Puerto Rico Song' plays from the car next to you and somehow it makes it worse and better simultaneously.

tldr: The US-Iran ceasefire is holding in name only as drones keep flying and missiles keep landing, while back home the Knicks are two wins from ending a 53-year championship drought and nobody can afford the gas to drive to the watch party.
